codeReview.summary: The refactor cleanly split `panel-status.ts` into focused modules while preserving the pre-refactor logic for status text updates, run timers, model catalog refresh, model selection, and the balance popover. All new status files are under the mission's 200-line limit, and I did not find a code-level regression against the feature intent or VAL-UI-005.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `refactor-worker` skill says final validation should include `npm run test:integration`, but this worker omitted that command and still reported `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true`. Either the skill should explicitly allow UI-only refactors to skip integration coverage, or workers should stop marking the procedure as followed when they skip that step.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/sero/projects/browser-ai/.factory/skills/refactor-worker/SKILL.md:96-101 requires `npm run test:unit`, `npm run test:integration`, `npm run lint`, `npm run check:repo-standards`, and `npm run build`; /Users/sero/.factory/missions/fdd24bf3-fc74-45b3-a646-4179615c1e31/handoffs/2026-03-11T13-03-29-372Z__sidepanel-refactor-panel-status__b7e16a16-cc2c-45bb-981b-859b5a8a84fd.json:16-36 lists only typecheck, test:unit, build, lint, and check:repo-standards.

sharedStateObservations.observation: This feature fulfills `VAL-UI-005`, whose evidence source is an e2e test, but the worker's recorded verification had no interactive or focused UI check. The shared guidance does not currently bridge that gap for status-panel refactors, so workers can complete an e2e-backed UI feature without any feature-specific runtime verification in the handoff.

summary: Reviewed the feature handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit `c6791d5`. The code split is structurally sound and preserves the status-panel behavior required by the feature; I found no blocking or non-blocking code issues, but I noted process gaps around missing integration/e2e-style verification in the worker procedure and handoff.
